conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you with your Child Benefit eligibility.

Let me start with a few questions. First:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 9 years old. I'd like to find out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'm happy to help you find out if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you some questions about your circumstances:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just one more question to complete my assessment:

**Are you or Alex's other parent (if applicable) responsible for Alex's day-to-day care - such as providing food, clothes, and looking after his general needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of that – his food, clothes, school supplies, everything really. He's my son and I take care of all his needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 9 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16)
- You live in the UK (Bristol) and Alex lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex's care and provide for all his needs (food, clothes, school supplies, etc.)
- Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim if you haven't already done so.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. You live in the UK (Bristol) and Alex lives with you. You are responsible for Alex's care, providing for his food, clothes, school supplies and all his general needs. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All of these conditions are met in your case, so you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Bristol) with their 9-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for all of Alex's care and daily needs, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, they live together in the UK, the claimant is responsible for the child's care, and only one person (the claimant) would be claiming. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All of these conditions are met in your case, so you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
